## [30:24 - 33:25]

So usually there's a two week ad timer on any ad, which means that if your ads currently crush in it and then after two weeks you see your cost per acquisition or cost per lead or cost per engagement start going up. It's because your ad is fatiguing, which means that you need different creative for the ad. Which means you got to swap out the image, swap out the video, swap out the headline, swap out the text that you use, swap out the newsfeed link description, swap out the call to action that you're using. You have to feed this Facebook algorithm monster a lot of different variations because it wants to achieve your objective and it wants to move fast and it wants to get your results that you desire. But it needs a lot of different variations in order to test what's best. And Facebook knows, they know this and they calculate this into the algorithm. The algorithm gets smarter every single day. That people do not want to see the same ad again and again and again. They don't want to see a stale ad. So that's what I mean by ad fatigue.
